history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible to the upper-right, but the cell directly in front of me contains lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The open floor is to my right, so I should turn right to face that safe path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing right. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The goal is up and to the left, and the cell above me is lava, so turning left would face danger. Turning right will face me downward, avoiding the immediate wall and lava while allowing me to navigate around the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing downward now. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is blocked. The visible safe floor extends to my left, while the right is blocked by a wall. Turning right will face me toward the open floor and let me continue exploring safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing left. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Behind that wall, the visible path continues on the other side, and the cell to my right is lava, so I should not turn around toward it. Turning left will face me downward, which is the only available direction to continue safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The open floor is to my left, while the right side is blocked. To align with the safe open path, I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The cell to one side is lava and should be avoided, while the other direction may allow progress. Turning left is the safest next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The open floor is to my left, while the right side is blocked by a wall. Turning right will reorient me toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The cell to one side is lava and should be avoided, while the other direction may allow safe progress. Turning left is the safest choice.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is blocked. The open floor is behind me relative to the corridor, and turning right will face that safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Lava is visible to one side, so I should avoid turning toward it. Turning left is the safer option to reorient toward a potentially open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so forward is blocked. The only visible safe corridor lies to my right, so I should rotate to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The open floor is on my right while lava is on my left, so turning right is the safe choice.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so I cannot move forward safely. There is an open floor cell to my right, so I should turn right to head toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead of me is floor, so I can safely advance. The goal is visible further ahead and to the left, and moving forward keeps me on the safe corridor toward it while avoiding the lava on my left.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the left. The cell directly in front of me is open floor, so moving forward is safe and brings me closer before turning toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
